title: quercus acutangula
intro: Quercus acutangula, also known as the sawtooth oak or cut-leaf oak, is a deciduous tree species native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus acutangula include its ability to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Quercus acutangula include decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are often used to make teas, tonics, and medicinal balms.
